201.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Umayyah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent him alone as a spy toward Quraysh. He said: 'I came to the cross on which Khubaib was mounted, and I was wary of lookouts. I climbed it and untied Khubaib, and he fell to the ground.'
201.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Umayyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mengutusnya seorang diri sebagai mata-mata ke arah Quraisy. Ia berkata: 'Aku mendatangi tiang salib tempat Khubaib disalib, sementara aku waspada terhadap para pengintai. Aku memanjatnya dan melepaskan Khubaib, sehingga ia jatuh ke tanah.'

202.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Umayyah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he saw the Prophet ﷺ wiping over his leather socks.
202.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Umayyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia melihat Nabi ﷺ mengusap di atas kedua khuf (sepatu kulit)nya.

203.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Umayyah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he saw the Prophet ﷺ eat a piece of meat in the prayer area and then pray without performing wudu.
203.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Umayyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia melihat Nabi ﷺ memakan sepotong daging di musalla lalu shalat tanpa berwudu.

204.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Umayyah al-Damri (may Allah be pleased with him) that they were with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on one of his journeys and they slept past the Fajr prayer until the sun rose. He said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ began with the two rak'ahs of sunnah, then prayed Fajr.'
204.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Umayyah al-Damri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa mereka bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dalam salah satu perjalanan dan mereka tertidur melewati waktu Subuh hingga matahari terbit. Ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ memulai dengan dua rakaat sunnah, kemudian shalat Subuh.'

205.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Umayyah al-Damri (may Allah be pleased with him) that he saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ wipe over his leather socks.
205.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Umayyah al-Damri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia melihat Rasulullah ﷺ mengusap di atas kedua khufnya.

206.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Umayyah al-Damri (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ wiping over his leather socks and turban.'
206.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Umayyah al-Damri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Rasulullah ﷺ mengusap di atas kedua khuf dan surbannya.'

207.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Umayyah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ wiping over his leather socks.'
207.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Umayyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Rasulullah ﷺ mengusap di atas kedua khufnya.'

208.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Umayyah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ cutting from a sheep's shoulder, then he was called to prayer, so he put down the knife and prayed without performing wudu.'
208.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Umayyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Rasulullah ﷺ sedang memotong dari paha depan domba, kemudian dipanggil untuk shalat, maka beliau meletakkan pisaunya dan shalat tanpa berwudu.'

209.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Umayyah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ eating and cutting from a shoulder, then he was called to prayer. He prayed without performing wudu.'
209.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Umayyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Rasulullah ﷺ makan dan memotong dari paha depan, kemudian dipanggil untuk shalat. Beliau shalat tanpa berwudu.'

210.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Umayyah al-Damri (may Allah be pleased with him) that he saw the Prophet ﷺ wiping over his leather socks.
210.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Umayyah al-Damri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia melihat Nabi ﷺ mengusap di atas kedua khufnya.

211.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Hawalah al-Azdi (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Syria will be opened for you, and Rome and Persia, until each one of you will have such-and-such camels, such-and-such cattle, such-and-such sheep — until one of them is given a hundred dinars and he is dissatisfied.' He then placed his hand on my head and said: 'O ibn Hawalah, when you see the caliphate has settled in the Holy Land, then earthquakes, trials, and great events are near.'
211.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Hawalah al-Azdi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Syam akan dibuka untuk kalian, dan Rum serta Persia, hingga salah seorang dari kalian memiliki sekian-sekian unta, sekian-sekian sapi, sekian-sekian kambing — hingga salah seorang dari mereka diberi seratus dinar lalu tidak puas.' Kemudian beliau meletakkan tangannya di kepalaku dan bersabda: 'Wahai ibn Hawalah, apabila engkau melihat khilafah telah menetap di Tanah Suci, maka gempa bumi, fitnah, dan peristiwa-peristiwa besar sudah dekat.'

212.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Hawalah al-Azdi (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever is saved from three things has truly been saved' — he said it three times. They said: 'From what, O Messenger of Allah?' He said: 'My death, the killing of a caliph who upholds the truth, and the Dajjal.'
212.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Hawalah al-Azdi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa selamat dari tiga hal, sungguh ia telah selamat' — beliau mengatakannya tiga kali. Mereka bertanya: 'Dari apa, wahai Rasulullah?' Beliau bersabda: 'Kematianku, pembunuhan seorang khalifah yang teguh dalam kebenaran, dan Dajjal.'

213.iwh_en: It was narrated from ibn Hawalah al-Azdi (may Allah be pleased with him) — who was a companion of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ — that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'There will be organized armies: Syria, Yemen, and Iraq — and Allah knows best which of them He will start with. But you must hold to Syria. You must hold to Syria. You must hold to Syria. Whoever does not want that, let him go to Yemen and draw water from its cisterns.'
213.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari ibn Hawalah al-Azdi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) — yang merupakan seorang sahabat Rasulullah ﷺ — bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Akan ada pasukan-pasukan yang terorganisir: Syam, Yaman, dan Irak — dan Allah lebih tahu mana yang akan Dia mulai. Tetapi kalian harus berpegang pada Syam. Kalian harus berpegang pada Syam. Kalian harus berpegang pada Syam. Siapa yang tidak menginginkan itu, hendaklah ia pergi ke Yaman dan mengambil air dari sumber-sumbernya.'

214.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ that the Prophet ﷺ was delivering a sermon when the killer said: 'O Messenger of Allah, by Allah, he only said what he said to seek protection from being killed.' The Prophet ﷺ turned away from him. The man called out again, and the Prophet ﷺ turned away again.
214.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat Rasulullah ﷺ bahwa Nabi ﷺ sedang berkhutbah ketika si pembunuh berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, demi Allah, ia hanya mengucapkan apa yang ia ucapkan untuk mencari perlindungan dari pembunuhan.' Nabi ﷺ berpaling darinya. Laki-laki itu memanggil lagi, dan Nabi ﷺ berpaling lagi.

215.iwh_en: It was narrated that al-Qasim, the freed servant of Mu'awiyah, entered the mosque of Damascus and saw people gathered around an old man who was narrating to them. He asked: 'Who is this?' They said: 'This is Sahl ibn al-Hanjaliyyah.' He heard him say: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: Whoever eats meat, let him perform wudu.'
215.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Qasim, bekas budak Mu'awiyah, bahwa ia masuk ke masjid Damaskus dan melihat orang-orang berkumpul di sekitar seorang syekh yang sedang bercerita kepada mereka. Ia bertanya: 'Siapa ini?' Mereka berkata: 'Ini adalah Sahl ibn al-Hanjaliyyah.' Ia mendengarnya berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: Barangsiapa memakan daging, hendaklah ia berwudu.'

216.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-Faghwa al-Khuza'i (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent him with a man and warned him: 'Beware of him, for he has entered the land of his people.' He said: When we reached al-Abwa', the man said: 'I need to attend to some business with my people in Waddan, so wait for me.' He turned and left, and I remembered the words of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, so I quickly departed.
216.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-Faghwa al-Khuza'i (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mengutusnya bersama seorang lelaki dan memperingatkan: 'Waspadalah terhadapnya, karena ia telah memasuki wilayah kaumnya.' Ia berkata: Ketika kami tiba di al-Abwa', lelaki itu berkata: 'Aku perlu mengurus sesuatu kepada kaumku di Waddan, tunggulah aku.' Ia pergi, dan aku teringat perkataan Rasulullah ﷺ, maka aku segera pergi.

217.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muhammad ibn 'Abdullah ibn Jahsh (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ shaded his eyes with his hand, then said: 'SubhanAllah, SubhanAllah! What severity has descended!' They remained silent that day and night and did not see it as something good. Muhammad said: 'I asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ what the severity was that had descended.' He said: 'Regarding debt. By the One in Whose hand my soul is, if a man were killed in the path of Allah, then came back to life, then was killed again, and had debt, he would not enter Paradise until his debt was paid.'
217.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Muhammad ibn 'Abdullah ibn Jahsh (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ memayungi matanya dengan tangannya, kemudian bersabda: 'Subhanallah, Subhanallah! Betapa beratnya yang telah turun!' Mereka terdiam sepanjang hari dan malam dan tidak melihatnya sebagai kebaikan. Muhammad berkata: 'Aku bertanya kepada Rasulullah ﷺ tentang beratnya apa yang telah turun itu.' Beliau bersabda: 'Tentang hutang. Demi Zat yang jiwaku berada di tangan-Nya, seandainya seseorang terbunuh di jalan Allah, lalu hidup kembali, lalu terbunuh lagi, dan ia memiliki hutang, ia tidak akan masuk surga sampai hutangnya dilunasi.'

218.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muhammad ibn Jahsh (may Allah be pleased with him), the brother-in-law of the Prophet ﷺ, that the Prophet ﷺ passed by Ma'mar in the courtyard of the mosque sitting with his thigh exposed, and the Prophet ﷺ said to him: 'O Ma'mar, cover your thigh, for the thigh is 'awrah.'
218.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Muhammad ibn Jahsh (radhiyallahu 'anhu), ipar Nabi ﷺ, bahwa Nabi ﷺ melewati Ma'mar di halaman masjid dalam keadaan duduk bersila dengan pahanya terbuka, maka Nabi ﷺ berkata kepadanya: 'Wahai Ma'mar, tutuplah pahamu, karena paha itu aurat.'

219.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muhammad ibn Jahsh (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ passed — and I was with him — by Ma'mar whose thighs were uncovered, and he said: 'O Ma'mar, cover your thighs, for the two thighs are 'awrah.'
219.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Muhammad ibn Jahsh (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ melewati — dan aku bersamanya — Ma'mar yang kedua pahanya terbuka, dan beliau bersabda: 'Wahai Ma'mar, tutuplah kedua pahamu, karena dua paha itu aurat.'

220.iwh_en: It was narrated from Samura ibn Sahm (may Allah be pleased with him) that he stayed with Abu Hashim ibn 'Utbah who was wounded with a spear. Mu'awiyah visited him and found him weeping. He said: 'O uncle, what makes you weep? Is it pain, or longing for the world?' He said: 'No, not either. Rather, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ had made a covenant with me, and I wish I had followed it. He ﷺ said: You may reach wealth that is distributed among peoples, and all you need from amassing wealth is a servant and a mount for the path of Allah.'
220.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Samurah ibn Sahm (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia menginap di rumah Abu Hashim ibn 'Utbah yang sedang terluka oleh tombak. Mu'awiyah mengunjunginya dan mendapatinya sedang menangis. Ia berkata: 'Wahai paman, apa yang membuatmu menangis? Apakah rasa sakit, atau kerinduan pada dunia?' Ia berkata: 'Tidak, bukan keduanya. Namun Rasulullah ﷺ pernah berwasiat kepadaku, dan aku berharap aku telah mengikutinya. Beliau ﷺ bersabda: Kamu mungkin akan menjumpai harta yang dibagi-bagikan di antara manusia, dan yang kamu perlukan dari mengumpulkan harta hanyalah seorang pelayan dan kendaraan di jalan Allah.'

221.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Harith ibn Ghutayf — or Ghutayf ibn al-Harith (may Allah be pleased with him) — that he said: 'Of all things I may have forgotten, I have not forgotten that I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ placing his right hand over his left in prayer.'
221.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Harith ibn Ghutayf — atau Ghutayf ibn al-Harith (radhiyallahu 'anhu) — bahwa ia berkata: 'Di antara hal-hal yang mungkin aku lupakan, aku tidak lupa bahwa aku melihat Rasulullah ﷺ meletakkan tangan kanannya di atas tangan kirinya dalam shalat.'

222.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm Salamah (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Negus asked Ja'far to recite something from what Allah had revealed. Ja'far recited the opening of Surah Maryam. The Negus wept, by Allah, until he soaked his beard, and his bishops wept until they soaked their scrolls.
222.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm Salamah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Najasyi meminta Ja'far untuk membacakan sesuatu dari apa yang Allah turunkan. Ja'far membacakan pembukaan Surah Maryam. Najasyi menangis, demi Allah, hingga membasahi janggutnya, dan para uskupnya menangis hingga membasahi lembaran-lembaran mereka.

223.iwh_en: It was narrated from Khalid ibn 'Arfatah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to him: 'O Khalid, there will be events, trials, and discord after me. If you are able to be the servant of Allah who is killed but does not kill, then do so.'
223.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Khalid ibn 'Arfatah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berkata kepadanya: 'Wahai Khalid, akan ada peristiwa-peristiwa, fitnah, dan perselisihan setelahku. Jika kamu bisa menjadi hamba Allah yang dibunuh tapi tidak membunuh, maka lakukanlah.'

224.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sulayman ibn Surad and Khalid ibn 'Arfatah (may Allah be pleased with them both) that they mentioned a man who had died of a stomach ailment and they intended to pray over him. One of them said to the other: 'Did not the Prophet ﷺ say: Whoever is killed by his stomach will not be tormented in his grave?' The other said: 'Yes.'
224.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sulayman ibn Surad dan Khalid ibn 'Arfatah (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa mereka menyebutkan seorang lelaki yang telah meninggal karena penyakit perut dan mereka bermaksud menshalatinya. Salah seorang berkata kepada yang lain: 'Bukankah Nabi ﷺ bersabda: Barangsiapa yang dibunuh oleh perutnya maka ia tidak akan disiksa di kuburnya?' Yang lain berkata: 'Ya.'

225.iwh_en: It was narrated from Khalid ibn 'Arfatah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said to al-Mukhtar: 'This man is a liar. I heard the Prophet ﷺ say: Whoever intentionally lies about me, let him take his seat in the Fire.'
225.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Khalid ibn 'Arfatah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata kepada al-Mukhtar: 'Lelaki ini adalah pendusta. Aku mendengar Nabi ﷺ bersabda: Barangsiapa berdusta atasku dengan sengaja, hendaklah ia mengambil tempatnya di neraka.'

226.iwh_en: It was narrated from Tariq ibn Suwayd al-Hadrami (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'O Messenger of Allah, in our land there are grapes that we press. May we drink from them?' He said: 'No.' He asked again, and he said: 'No.' He asked again and said: 'We use it as medicine for the sick.' He said: 'It is not a medicine; it is a disease.'
226.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Tariq ibn Suwayd al-Hadrami (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, di tanah kami ada anggur yang kami peras. Bolehkah kami meminumnya?' Beliau berkata: 'Tidak.' Ia bertanya lagi, dan beliau berkata: 'Tidak.' Ia bertanya lagi dan berkata: 'Kami menggunakannya sebagai obat untuk orang sakit.' Beliau bersabda: 'Itu bukan obat; itu penyakit.'

227.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Hisham (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and 'Umar ibn al-Khattab was holding his hand. 'Umar said: 'O Messenger of Allah, you are dearer to me than everything except myself.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'No, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, until I am dearer to you than yourself.' 'Umar said: 'Now, by Allah, you are dearer to me than myself.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Now, O 'Umar.'
227.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Hisham (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dan 'Umar ibn al-Khattab memegang tangannya. 'Umar berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, engkau lebih kucintai dari segalanya kecuali diriku sendiri.' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidak, demi Zat yang jiwaku berada di tangan-Nya, hingga aku lebih kamu cintai dari dirimu sendiri.' 'Umar berkata: 'Sekarang, demi Allah, engkau lebih kucintai dari diriku sendiri.' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sekarang baru benar, wahai 'Umar.'

228.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Hisham (may Allah be pleased with him) that he reached puberty during the time of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and married women.
228.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Hisham (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia mencapai kedewasaan pada masa Rasulullah ﷺ dan telah menikah.

229.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Sa'd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he asked the Prophet ﷺ about eating with a menstruating woman. He said: 'Eat with her.'
229.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Sa'd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ tentang makan bersama wanita yang sedang haid. Beliau bersabda: 'Makanlah bersamanya.'

230.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion (may Allah be pleased with him) who heard the Prophet ﷺ say: 'People will not be destroyed until they have no excuse left for themselves.'
230.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat (radhiyallahu 'anhu) yang mendengar Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Manusia tidak akan binasa hingga mereka tidak memiliki alasan lagi untuk diri mereka sendiri.'

231.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion (may Allah be pleased with him) who heard the Prophet ﷺ say: 'Know that a borrowed item must be returned, a gift given temporarily must be returned, a debt must be repaid, and a guarantor is liable.'
231.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat (radhiyallahu 'anhu) yang mendengar Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Ketahuilah bahwa barang pinjaman wajib dikembalikan, hadiah sementara wajib dikembalikan, hutang wajib dibayar, dan penjamin wajib menanggung.'

232.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umayyah al-Makhzumi (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said regarding a thief: 'Cut off his hand.' Then they brought the thief and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to him: 'Say: I seek forgiveness from Allah and I repent to Him.' He said it, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'O Allah, forgive him.'
232.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umayyah al-Makhzumi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda tentang seorang pencuri: 'Potonglah tangannya.' Kemudian mereka membawa pencuri itu dan Rasulullah ﷺ berkata kepadanya: 'Ucapkanlah: Aku memohon ampun kepada Allah dan aku bertobat kepada-Nya.' Ia mengucapkannya, dan Rasulullah ﷺ berdoa: 'Ya Allah, ampunilah dia.'

233.iwh_en: It was narrated from a man of the Ansar (may Allah be pleased with him) that they went out with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and he took a piece of meat and spat it out. He said: 'I find the meat of a sheep taken without the permission of its owners.' A woman stood up and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, I wanted to gather you and those with you for a meal, so I sent to al-Baqi' but found no sheep for sale.'
233.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang lelaki dari kaum Anshar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa mereka keluar bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dan beliau mengambil sepotong daging lalu memuntahkannya. Beliau bersabda: 'Aku menemukan daging domba yang diambil tanpa izin pemiliknya.' Seorang wanita berdiri dan berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, aku ingin mengumpulkan engkau dan orang-orang yang bersamamu untuk makan, maka aku mengirim ke al-Baqi' tapi tidak menemukan domba yang dijual.'

234.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Sawwar (may Allah be pleased with him) through his maternal uncle that he saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and Gabriel, peace be upon him, descended upon the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'You are a shepherd; do not break the horns of your flock.' When they prayed the morning prayer, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'O Allah, I am a shepherd...'
234.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Sawwar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui paman dari pihak ibunya bahwa ia melihat Rasulullah ﷺ dan Jibril 'alaihis salam turun kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: 'Engkau adalah seorang gembala; jangan patahkan tanduk kawananmu.' Ketika mereka menunaikan shalat Subuh, Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ya Allah, aku adalah seorang gembala...'

235.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Shahm (may Allah be pleased with him) that a young girl passed by him in Madinah and he grabbed her waist. He came to the Prophet ﷺ who refused to accept his pledge of allegiance. Someone said: 'The one who grabbed her waist has come.' He said: 'By Allah, I will never do it again.' So the Prophet ﷺ took his pledge.
235.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Shahm (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang gadis melewatinya di Madinah dan ia memegang pinggangnya. Ia mendatangi Nabi ﷺ yang menolak menerima bai'atnya. Seseorang berkata: 'Yang memegang pinggang gadis itu telah datang.' Ia berkata: 'Demi Allah, aku tidak akan mengulanginya.' Maka Nabi ﷺ menerima bai'atnya.

236.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Shahm (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I was a dissolute man. A young girl passed by me in Madinah, so I grabbed her.' He came to the Prophet ﷺ while the people were pledging allegiance and extended his hand. The Prophet ﷺ pulled his hand back and said: 'I think you are the one who grabbed the girl yesterday.' He said: 'O Messenger of Allah, take my pledge. By Allah, I will never do it again.' He said: 'Very well then.'
236.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Shahm (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku adalah seorang yang suka berbuat sia-sia. Seorang gadis melewatiku di Madinah, maka aku memegang pinggangnya.' Ia mendatangi Nabi ﷺ saat orang-orang sedang berbai'at dan mengulurkan tangannya. Nabi ﷺ menarik tangannya dan berkata: 'Kukira kamu adalah orang yang kemarin memegang gadis itu.' Ia berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, ambillah bai'atku. Demi Allah, aku tidak akan mengulanginya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Baiklah kalau begitu.'

237.iwh_en: It was narrated from Makhariق ibn Shu'bah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: 'What if a man comes wanting to take my wealth?' He said: 'Invoke Allah upon him.' He said: 'And if I do that and he does not stop?' He said: 'Seek help from the authority.' He said: 'And if there is no one from them nearby?' He said: 'Strive against him or fight him until you are recorded among the martyrs of the Hereafter, or you protect your wealth.'
237.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Makhariq ibn Shu'bah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang lelaki mendatangi Rasulullah ﷺ dan berkata: 'Bagaimana jika seorang lelaki datang hendak mengambil hartaku?' Beliau bersabda: 'Agungkan Allah kepadanya.' Ia berkata: 'Bagaimana jika aku melakukan itu dan ia tidak berhenti?' Beliau bersabda: 'Mintalah bantuan kepada penguasa.' Ia berkata: 'Bagaimana jika tidak ada seorang pun dari mereka di dekatku?' Beliau bersabda: 'Berjuanglah melawannya atau perangilah hingga kamu dicatat di antara para syuhada akhirat, atau kamu berhasil melindungi hartamu.'

238.iwh_en: It was narrated from Makhariq ibn Shu'bah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'What if a man comes to take my wealth?' He said: 'If he does not stop, seek help from the authority.' He said: 'What if the authority is far from me?' He said: 'Seek help from the Muslims.' He said: 'What if none of the Muslims are present and he comes at me quickly?' He said: 'Then fight until you protect your wealth or you are killed and become among the martyrs of the Hereafter.'
238.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Makhariq ibn Shu'bah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang lelaki mendatangi Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: 'Bagaimana jika seorang lelaki datang untuk mengambil hartaku?' Beliau bersabda: 'Jika ia tidak berhenti, mintalah bantuan kepada penguasa.' Ia berkata: 'Bagaimana jika penguasa jauh dariku?' Beliau bersabda: 'Mintalah bantuan kepada kaum Muslim.' Ia berkata: 'Bagaimana jika tidak ada seorang pun dari kaum Muslim hadir dan ia menyerangku dengan cepat?' Beliau bersabda: 'Maka perangilah hingga kamu melindungi hartamu atau kamu terbunuh dan menjadi di antara para syuhada akhirat.'

239.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu 'Uqbah (may Allah be pleased with him) — who was a freed servant of Persian origin — that he was with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on the Day of Uhud and struck a polytheist, saying: 'Take that from me — I am the Persian boy!' This reached the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'Why did you not say: Take that from me — I am the Ansari boy!'
239.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu 'Uqbah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) — yang merupakan bekas budak keturunan Persia — bahwa ia bersama Rasulullah ﷺ pada Perang Uhud dan memukul seorang musyrik sambil berkata: 'Terimalah ini dariku — aku adalah bocah Persia!' Hal ini sampai kepada Nabi ﷺ yang bersabda: 'Mengapa kamu tidak berkata: Terimalah ini dariku — aku adalah bocah Anshar!'

240.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion of the Prophet ﷺ (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard the Prophet ﷺ say: 'When one of you is in prayer, let him not raise his gaze to the sky lest his sight be snatched away.'
240.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat Nabi ﷺ (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia mendengar Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila salah seorang dari kalian sedang shalat, janganlah ia mengangkat pandangannya ke langit agar penglihatannya tidak disambar.'

241.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was asked about fasting on the Day of 'Arafah. He said: 'It expiates two years.' He was asked about fasting on the Day of 'Ashura' and he said: 'It expiates one year.'
241.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ditanya tentang puasa pada Hari Arafah. Beliau bersabda: 'Ia menghapus dosa dua tahun.' Beliau ditanya tentang puasa pada Hari Asyura dan beliau bersabda: 'Ia menghapus dosa satu tahun.'

242.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever establishes proof over a slain person, he is entitled to the spoils of that slain person.'
242.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang membuktikan bahwa ia yang membunuh seseorang, maka baginya hak atas barang rampasan orang yang terbunuh itu.'

243.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ praying while carrying Umamah — or Umayymah — daughter of Abi al-'As, who was the daughter of Zaynab. He carried her when he stood and put her down when he bowed, until he finished.'
243.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Rasulullah ﷺ shalat sambil menggendong Umamah — atau Umaimah — putri Abi al-'As, yang merupakan putri Zainab. Beliau menggendongnya ketika berdiri dan meletakkannya ketika ruku, hingga selesai.'

244.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to lead us in prayer, reciting in the first two rak'ahs of the Zuhr prayer and letting us hear a verse occasionally. He would lengthen the first rak'ah and shorten the second. He used to do that in the Fajr prayer as well.'
244.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa mengimami kami dalam shalat, membaca dalam dua rakaat pertama shalat Dhuhur dan sesekali memperdengarkan ayat kepada kami. Beliau memanjangkan rakaat pertama dan memendekkan rakaat kedua. Beliau juga melakukan itu dalam shalat Subuh.'

245.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet of Allah ﷺ forbade mixing two types of drink together, but each one should be steeped separately.
245.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi Allah ﷺ melarang mencampurkan dua jenis minuman, melainkan masing-masingnya harus direndam secara terpisah.

246.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ forbade breathing into a vessel, touching one's private part with the right hand, and cleaning oneself with the right hand.
246.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ melarang bernapas ke dalam wadah, menyentuh kemaluan dengan tangan kanan, dan beristinja dengan tangan kanan.

247.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When one of you enters the mosque, let him pray two rak'ahs before sitting.'
247.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila salah seorang dari kalian masuk masjid, hendaklah ia shalat dua rakaat sebelum duduk.'

248.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to pray while carrying Umamah bint Zaynab. When he bowed and prostrated, he would put her down; and when he stood, he would carry her.
248.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa shalat sambil menggendong Umamah binti Zainab. Ketika ruku dan sujud, beliau meletakkannya; dan ketika berdiri, beliau menggendongnya.

249.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'The true dream is from Allah, and the bad dream is from Satan. Whoever sees a dream he dislikes, let him not tell anyone about it, and let him spit to his left three times and seek refuge in Allah from its evil. Then it will not harm him.'
249.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Mimpi yang benar adalah dari Allah, dan mimpi buruk adalah dari setan. Barangsiapa melihat mimpi yang tidak ia sukai, janganlah ia menceritakannya kepada siapa pun, dan hendaklah ia meludah ke kirinya tiga kali dan berlindung kepada Allah dari keburukannya. Maka mimpi itu tidak akan membahayakannya.'

250.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he hunted a wild donkey while he was not in ihram and the others were in ihram. They asked the Prophet ﷺ about it, and he commanded them to eat it.
250.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berburu keledai liar sementara ia tidak dalam keadaan ihram dan yang lainnya sedang ihram. Mereka bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ tentang hal itu, dan beliau memerintahkan mereka untuk memakannya.
